sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Gilead and South Africa are negotiating a license for local production of new HIV drug
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences is negotiating a voluntary license with the South African government to allow local production of its new HIV prevention drug, lenacapavir. This is significant because South Africa has the world's highest HIV burden, and the drug, which offers virtually complete protection with bi-annual injections, has generated considerable interest among health policymakers. The country is collaborating with Unitaid and US Pharmacopeia to identify suitable local manufacturers.

feed.title: Investors value Manhattan office real estate for less than they did in 2020 amid AI anxiety
feed.summary: Manhattan office real estate, including properties owned by SL Green, Vornado Realty Trust, and Empire State Realty Trust, is currently valued by investors at levels below those seen in June 2020. This decline is attributed to fears that artificial intelligence will reduce demand for office space by making a significant portion of the white-collar workforce redundant. Despite some positive indicators like a recovering office market in New York City and increased subway ridership, recent data on office leasing and property valuations suggest a continued downturn for the sector due to AI-driven anxiety.

feed.title: UNIFI launches Luxel yarn
feed.summary: UNIFI has introduced Luxel, a new yarn technology that mimics the look and feel of linen while offering enhanced performance and sustainability. Made with REPREVE recycled polyester, including 30% REPREVE Takeback circular polyester from textile waste, Luxel provides features like moisture management, wrinkle resistance, and odor control. This innovation by UNIFI highlights its commitment to circularity and aims to provide brands with a versatile, eco-conscious material for various applications.

feed.title: 100 chemical CEOs head to Houston to rewrite the petrochemicals playbook
feed.summary: S&P Global will host its 41st World Petrochemical Conference (WPC) in Houston from March 23-27, 2026, bringing together over 1,200 participants, including 100 CEOs from more than 50 countries. The conference, themed "Catalyzing the Transformation: Renewal for Chemicals in an Era of Disruption," will address critical industry challenges such as supply chain discipline, feedstock shifts, decarbonization strategies, and regional demand dynamics. Speakers will include top executives from major chemical companies like Dow, LyondellBasell, ExxonMobil, and Chevron Phillips Chemical Company.

feed.title: Walmart Offers Free AI Training to 1.6 Million Employees. It’s a Genius Move
feed.summary: Walmart is providing free AI training to its entire U.S. workforce of 1.6 million employees, partnering with Google and OpenAI for certification programs. This initiative is seen as a strategic move to aid recruiting and retention, build in-house AI expertise, and manage technological change effectively. While some speculate about potential layoffs, the article argues this approach will help employees adapt to AI-driven changes and enhance the company's capabilities.
